internal_issues.description: Framing inconsistency between HTML and DOCX: The DOCX (build-doc-v4.js line 495) frames WIN-056 as: 'H(r) was fitted to match solar observations, then solar elevation is re-derived from H(r). This is circular.' The HTML detail focuses on globe formula substitution (23.45° axial tilt). These are rela

WIN-057 and WIN-058 are conflated in the build doc. The build-doc-v4.js file states: 'WIN-057/058 (two-zone disc / angular coordinate): V13 two-zone topology improved cross-equatorial routes but regressed northern hemisphere accuracy from 5.2% to 7.3%. An internal coordinate convention is not a phys

Section numbering mismatch between HTML and DOCX in Part 4.5: HTML 4.5.3 = Gravity at Rim, HTML 4.5.4 = Solar Diameter. DOCX 4.5.3 = Solar Angular Diameter, DOCX 4.5.4 = Tidal Pattern. Kill-shot table cross-reference row 11 references Section 4.5.3 — broken in one format. Curmudgeon review: SEC-4.5.3/4.5.4.

DOCX MISSING SECTIONS 5.14-5.16: The Word/PDF document (build-doc-v4.js) completely omits Sections 5.14 (Repository Infrastructure: Steering AI), 5.15 (The Repository Is the Model), and 5.16 (The Monitoring Infrastructure). These three sections contain the review's most original contribution — the inject_ai_layer.py analysis, curve-fitting exposure, and blockchain timestamping critique. Any reader using the Word/PDF document misses the entire repository code analysis argument. This has been flagged by the curmudgeon in 4 consecutive section reviews (SEC-5.14, 5.15, 5.16, and repeated in 5.16 with priority escalation to critical). Requires adding Sections 5.14-5.16 to build-doc-v4.js.

DOME SOURCE FILES NOT ARCHIVED: Sections 5.14 and 5.16 make specific claims about Python scripts (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py, monitor.py, recalc_v51.py, app.py, test_curve_stretching.py, find_curve.py) including exact quotes, line references, and formula citations. None of these files are preserved in raw-text/. If the dome author modifies or removes these scripts, our claims become unsubstantiated assertions — especially ironic given Section 5.16 criticizes the dome for not archiving its predictions. The -29.1 nT calculation in Section 5.16 cites monitor.py which is unarchived, making the specific formula unverifiable. Curmudgeon flagged this as the highest-priority unresolved citation issue.

DOCX Self-Contradicted count hardcoded as 11 in build-doc-v4.js (Pattern 11 text line ~791, summary paragraph line ~812, version history line ~859), but wins.json now has 12 Self-Contradicted WINs. The HTML correctly uses computed counts. Violates project rule on hardcoding counts.

SEC-6.2 DOCX: Curmudgeon reports cross-reference error - DOCX version references "Part 3.1" instead of "Section 3.2" for eclipse analysis. HTML version correct. Fix: Check build-doc-v4.js eclipse conclusion paragraph for Part 3.1 and update to Section 3.2.

KILLSHOT-GAIA missing from DOCX/PDF: Section 4.3 (Gaia kill-shot) appears in HTML but build-doc-v4.js has no corresponding section. The Word/PDF download is missing one of the six kill-shots entirely. Needs a new section added to build-doc-v4.js after the GPS kill-shot.

WIN-058 verdict mismatch: Currently classified 'Misleading' but detail_verdict_text describes it as 'Internal coordinate convention, not a physical prediction' which logically fits 'Not Demonstrated' better. If the argument is that dome's two-zone coordinate system is a mathematical curve-fit with no physical justification, that's an unfalsifiable convention (Not Demonstrated), not a misrepresentation (Misleading). Either change verdict to 'Not Demonstrated' or revise finding language to emphasize curve-fitting deception rather than mere convention.

VERDICT LEGEND ORDERING ARBITRARY: Current verdict order in legend appears unsystematic. Recommend ordering by evidential force: (1) Wrong on Own Terms (Self-Contradicted, Refuted by Data) → (2) Not Evidence (Std Model Explains, Misleading) → (3) Not Evaluable (Not Demonstrated, Unfalsifiable). This grouping reflects logical hierarchy: Can the dome contradict itself? → Can the dome offer new evidence? → Can the dome be tested? Current legend order does not reflect this structure.

HOL-HIERARCHY (curmudgeon alert 2026-04-07T15:30): Argument hierarchy is inverted for persuasive force. Self-contradiction arguments (Schumann WIN-002, Tidal WIN-045/046/049/050/051, Gravity at rim WIN-067) are in Section 4.5 (Internal Contradictions tab, 8th of 10 tabs). External falsification arguments (weaker — vulnerable to frame-of-reference objections) are in Part 4 (tabs 2-3). Readers encounter the weaker external arguments first and may never reach the dome's own geometry refuting itself. Restructuring Part 4 to lead with self-contradictions would significantly improve persuasive impact without changing any content.

WIN-041: The 28 nT/year threshold used to validate the claim is a hardcoded model input (raw-text/05-model.txt line 42: 'Field decay rate: >=28 nT/year'), not derived from dome geometry. The review should note this explicitly — setting your own threshold and then confirming the observation meets it is circular.
